commit.message:
fix: resolve Test262 stack overflow — run on main thread (#499)

Diagnostics proved the crash occurs in the test execution loop, not in
install_globals. Spawned pthreads get fixed-size mmap'd stacks while
the main thread's stack is dynamically growable under ulimit -s unlimited.

Changes:
- Run main_inner() directly on the main thread instead of spawning
- Keep stacker::maybe_grow(2GiB, 32MiB) for install_globals
- opt-level=1 for stator_core and stator_test262
- lto=false to prevent cross-crate frame consolidation
- RUST_MIN_STACK=1GiB in CI for internal spawns
- Cargo.toml added to test262 workflow path triggers

commit.message:
Fix empty benchmark results: NIST CTC manifest YAML block scalar breaks custom parser (#42)

* Initial plan

* Fix empty benchmark results: convert NIST CTC manifest summary from YAML block scalar to single-line string

The custom YAML subset parser in fixture_manifest.cc does not support
YAML block scalars (> or |). The nist-ctc/manifest.yaml used:

  summary: >
    NIST MBE PMI Conformance Test Case ...

The parser reads the second indented line and throws "Unexpected
indentation", which bench_navigator catches as a hard manifest parse
error. Because HasErrors() returns true, bench_navigator exits with
EXIT_FAILURE before printing the "=== Fixture Ray Benchmark Results ==="
header. The generate_bench_report.py parser finds no header and renders
an empty page.

Fix: convert the summary to a single-line quoted string, consistent with
all other family manifests (boolean-compound, direct-primitives,
profile-faceted, tessellated, twisted-swept, wrapper-decorator).

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: discover shipyard ports when over fleet target

Previously knownShipyardPortIds started empty, so the relocation block
never tagged any ships (gated on knownYards.length > 0) — chicken-and-egg.

Now: when over target, probe up to 20 unique docked-ship port IDs in
parallel each cycle to find shipyards. Discovered ports are added to
knownShipyardPortIds so relocation tagging and direct sells can proceed.
Ports already in the known list are skipped.

commit.message:
fix(destroy): fix TrailLogsBucket deletion — wrong bucket name in AWS_03 destroy

Root cause: AWS_03 destroy workflow was constructing the bucket name as
  ${PROJECT}-${ENV}-${ACCOUNT_ID}-cloudtrail
but the observability.yaml template creates it as:
  ${ProjectName}-${Environment}-cloudtrail-logs-${AWS::AccountId}
The suffix order and value were both wrong so the empty-bucket step silently
skipped the bucket, leaving objects inside, causing CF DELETE_FAILED.

Fix: get bucket name directly from the live CloudFormation stack resource
using describe-stack-resource on logical ID 'TrailLogsBucket'. This is
robust — if the bucket name ever changes in the template the destroy still
works correctly.

Also improved: handles versioned objects + delete markers in a single
loop, cleaner null checks, explicit success log message.

Re-run the AWS_03 Destroy workflow — the stack is in DELETE_FAILED state
which the workflow handles (condition is != NOT_FOUND).

commit.message:
ble: parse HR, cadence, stride and time from psmh GPS detail

Extends the psmh parser to extract all available per-point and
run-level data from the sports-detail BLE transfer:

Per GPS point (populated in RoutePoint.hr / .cad / .t):
- Type 8 (HEARTRATE): carry-forward hr_bpm to each GPS_DELTA point
- Type 5 (SPEED):     carry-forward cadence_spm to each GPS_DELTA point
- Type 1 (TIMESTAMP): track Unix-ms anchor for per-point time
- Type 3 (GPS_DELTA): compute t = (anchor_ms + offset_ms - runStartMs)/1000

Run-level averages (sent in PATCH body, stored in DB):
- avg_cadence: mean of all SPEED record cadence_spm values
- avg_stride_m: mean of all SPEED record stride_cm / 100 values

Backend (patch_ble_route):
- BleRouteInput now accepts avg_cadence and avg_stride_m
- Computes and stores elevation_gain_m from route point elevations
- One UPDATE sets route_json, elevation_gain_m, avg_cadence, avg_stride_m

This enables lap splits (pace, HR, cadence, stride per km), the pace
chart, stride chart, and cadence trend — all already implemented in
the Flutter UI and computed from per-point data.

commit.message:
ble: rewrite parseGpsDetail to decode psmh binary format

Replace the incorrect protobuf-based GPS decoder with a correct
implementation for the Huami2021 'psmh' binary activity detail format.

The format is a 18-byte header (magic + flags + timestamp) followed by
TLV records. Decoded record types:
- Type 2 (GPS_COORDS, len=20): sets absolute lon/lat anchor
  [6B skip][int32 LE lon][int32 LE lat][6B skip]
- Type 3 (GPS_DELTA, len=8): increments lon/lat raw integers
  [int16 LE offset][int16 LE lon_delta][int16 LE lat_delta][int16 const=2]
- Type 7 (ALTITUDE, len=6): current GPS altitude in centimetres
  [int16 LE offset][int32 LE alt_cm]

Coordinate conversion: decimal_degrees = huami_int32 / 3_000_000.0
(formula from Gadgetbridge AbstractHuamiActivityDetailsParser.java)

Removes the now-unused _tryGpsPoint() and _zigzagDecode() helpers.
Adds gps_parser_test.dart covering: empty input, non-psmh data,
correct coordinate decoding, altitude, and no-anchor guard.

commit.message:
fix(core-infra): remove GroupName from RDS and Redis security groups

GroupName causes 'already exists' failures when a previous stack run
left orphaned security groups behind. Without GroupName, CloudFormation
auto-generates a unique ID and never conflicts on re-creation.

The Name tag still provides the human-readable label in the AWS console.
This is the correct CloudFormation pattern for resources that may need
to be recreated across multiple stack runs.

fix(aws02): fix orphaned SG detection - check if owning stack is still alive

Bug: aws:cloudformation:stack-name tag persists on SGs even after the
owning stack is deleted. The previous cleanup code saw the tag matching
the current stack name and skipped deletion, but the stack had been
deleted - leaving the SG as an orphan that blocks re-creation.

Fix: after finding the stack tag, call describe-stacks to verify the
tagged stack is actually alive (CREATE_COMPLETE / UPDATE_COMPLETE /
UPDATE_ROLLBACK_COMPLETE). If the stack is deleted, failed, or does
not exist, the SG is treated as orphaned and deleted.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add comprehensive Playwright E2E test that plays through 4 in-game days:

synthetic browser events from Playwright.

feat: background KPI/ships fetch with skeleton loading + server cache

- New GET /api/dashboard/kpis: reads autopilot state from MongoDB,
  computes cargoInTransitValue / paxInTransit / cargoAtCost / netProfit
  — no upstream API calls, near-instant response

- New GET /api/dashboard/ships?page=N&limit=50: server-side paginated
  ships list with 18s in-memory cache (one cache entry per companyId).
  ~9 upstream paginated calls happen server-to-server once, then all
  browser requests within 18s return cached JSON. Clicking Next/Prev
  pages through the cache without hitting the upstream at all.

  economy, ledger, ports, goods, warehouse stocks) — no more blocking

fix(aws02): auto-clean orphaned security groups before deployment

Previous failed stack runs leave security groups behind that were
not deleted during rollback (due to timing or dependency ordering).
CloudFormation fails with 'already exists' when re-creating them.

Added 'Pre-flight cleanup of orphaned security groups' step (step 6)
that runs after the stack state check but before deployment:
- Looks up each expected SG by name + VPC ID
- Checks the aws:cloudformation:stack-name tag to determine ownership
- Skips SGs owned by another active stack (safe guard)
- Skips SGs owned by the current stack (CFN handles update)
- Deletes SGs with no stack owner (orphaned from deleted stack runs)

Affected SGs: ${PROJECT_NAME}-${ENV}-sg-rds and -sg-redis
